sections.paragraph:
κατιόω (s. ἰός 2; Sir 12:11) pf. pass. 3 sg. κατίωται; pass. in intr. sense become rusty, tarnished, corroded (Strabo 16, 2, 42; Epict. 4, 6, 14) of gold and silver (cp. EpJer 10) Js 5:3.—DELG 4 ἰός. TW.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

intr. intr. = intransitive

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 534
